esrs.topicName: Scope of consolidation of consolidated sustainability statement is same as for financial statements
esrs.requirementText: a confirmation that the scope of consolidation is the same as for the financial statements, or, where applicable, a declaration that the reporting undertaking is not required to draw-up financial statements or that the reporting undertaking is preparing consolidated sustainability reporting pursuant to Article 48i of Directive 2013/34/EU; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: a confirmation that the scope of consolidation is the same as for the financial statements, or, where applicable, a declaration that the reporting undertaking is not required to draw-up financial statements or that the reporting undertaking is preparing consolidated sustainability reporting pursuant to Article 48i of Directive 2013/34/EU; and

esrs.requirementText: where applicable, an indication of which subsidiary undertakings included in the consolidation are exempted from individual or consolidated sustainability reporting pursuant to Articles 19a(9) or 29a(8) of Directive 2013/34/EU;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: where applicable, an indication of which subsidiary undertakings included in the consolidation are exempted from individual or consolidated sustainability reporting pursuant to Articles 19a(9) or 29a(8) of Directive 2013/34/EU;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of extent to which sustainability statement covers upstream and downstream value chain [text block]

esrs.disclosureExpectation: to what extent the sustainability statement covers the undertaking’s upstream and downstream value chain (see ESRS 1 section 5.1 Reporting undertaking and value chain );

esrs.topicName: Either option to omit specific piece of information corresponding to intellectual property, know-how or results of innovation has been used

esrs.disclosureExpectation: whether the undertaking has used the option to omit a specific piece of information corresponding to intellectual property, know-how or the results of innovation (see ESRS 1 section 7.7 Classified and sensitive information and information on intellectual property, know-how or results of innovation ); and

esrs.topicName: Option allowed by Member State to omit disclosure of impending developments or matters in course of negotiation has been used
esrs.requirementText: for undertakings based in an EU member state that allows for the exemption from disclosure of impending developments or matters in the course of negotiation, as provided for in articles 19a(3) and 29a(3) of Directive 2013/34/EU, whether the undertaking has used that exemption.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: for undertakings based in an EU member state that allows for the exemption from disclosure of impending developments or matters in the course of negotiation, as provided for in articles 19a(3) and 29a(3) of Directive 2013/34/EU, whether the undertaking has used that exemption.

esrs.topicName: European standards approved by European Standardisation System (ISO/IEC or CEN/CENELEC standards) have been relied on
esrs.requirementText: AR 2. The undertaking may disclose whether it relies on any European standards approved by the European Standardisation System (ISO/IEC or CEN/CENELEC standards), as well as the extent to which data and processes that are used for sustainability reporting purposes have been verified by an external assurance provider and found to conform to the corresponding ISO/IEC or CEN/CENELEC standard.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 2. The undertaking may disclose whether it relies on any European standards approved by the European Standardisation System (ISO/IEC or CEN/CENELEC standards), as well as the extent to which data and processes that are used for sustainability reporting purposes have been verified by an external assurance provider and found to conform to the corresponding ISO/IEC or CEN/CENELEC standard.

esrs.topicName: List of sustainability matter(s) assessed to be material (phase-in)
esrs.requirementText: [Main] When performing its materiality assessment, the undertaking shall consider the following list of sustainability matters covered in the topical ESRS. When, as a result of the undertaking’s materiality assessment (see ESRS 2 IRO-1), a given sustainability matter in this list is assessed to be material, the undertaking shall report according to the corresponding Disclosure Requirements of the relevant topical ESRS. Using this list is not a substitute for the process of determining material matters. This list...

esrs.requirementText: disclose the list of matters (i.e. topic, sub-topic or sub-sub-topic) in AR 16 ESRS 1 Appendix A that are assessed to be material and briefly describe how the undertaking’s business model and strategy take account of the impacts of the undertaking related to those matters. This may be presented as a content index. The undertaking shall also include a table of all the datapoints that derive from other EU legislation as listed in Appendix B of this standard, indi...

esrs.topicName: List of not material topic(s)
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 57. If the undertaking concludes that climate change is not material and therefore omits all disclosure requirements in ESRS E1 Climate change , it shall disclose a detailed explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment with regard to climate change (see ESRS 2 IRO-2 Disclosure Requirements in ESRS covered by the undertaking’s sustainability statement ), including a forward-looking analysis of the conditions that could lead the undertaking to conclude that climate change is material in the...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] 57. If the undertaking concludes that climate change is not material and therefore omits all disclosure requirements in ESRS E1 Climate change , it shall disclose a detailed explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment with regard to climate change (see ESRS 2 IRO-2 Disclosure Requirements in ESRS covered by the undertaking’s sustainability statement ), including a forward-looking analysis of the conditions that could lead the undertaking to conclude that climate change is material in the...

esrs.topicName: Explanation of negative materiality assessment for ESRS E1 Climate change [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 57. If the undertaking concludes that climate change is not material and therefore omits all disclosure requirements in ESRS E1 Climate change , it shall disclose a detailed explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment with regard to climate change (see ESRS 2 IRO-2 Disclosure Requirements in ESRS covered by the undertaking’s sustainability statement ), including a forward-looking analysis of the conditions that could lead the undertaking to conclude that climate change is material in the future.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 57. If the undertaking concludes that climate change is not material and therefore omits all disclosure requirements in ESRS E1 Climate change , it shall disclose a detailed explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment with regard to climate change (see ESRS 2 IRO-2 Disclosure Requirements in ESRS covered by the undertaking’s sustainability statement ), including a forward-looking analysis of the conditions that could lead the undertaking to conclude that climate change is material in the future.

esrs.topicName: Explanation of negative materiality assessment for ESRS E2 Pollution [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 58. If the undertaking concludes that a topic other than climate change is not material and therefore omits all the Disclosure Requirements in the corresponding topical ESRS, it may provide a brief explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment for that topic.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 58. If the undertaking concludes that a topic other than climate change is not material and therefore omits all the Disclosure Requirements in the corresponding topical ESRS, it may provide a brief explanation of the conclusions of its materiality assessment for that topic.

esrs.topicName: Explanation of how material information to be disclosed in relation to material impacts, risks and opportunities has been determined [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 59. The undertaking shall provide an explanation of how it has determined the material information to be disclosed in relation to the impacts, risks and opportunities that it has assessed to be material, including the use of thresholds and/or how it has implemented the criteria in ESRS 1 section 3.2 Material matters and materiality of information.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 59. The undertaking shall provide an explanation of how it has determined the material information to be disclosed in relation to the impacts, risks and opportunities that it has assessed to be material, including the use of thresholds and/or how it has implemented the criteria in ESRS 1 section 3.2 Material matters and materiality of information.

esrs.clauseId: ESRS 2 Minimum disclosure requirements on policies and actions 62
esrs.topicName: Sustainability matter(s) for which policies have not been adopted
esrs.requirementText: 62. If the undertaking cannot disclose the information on policies and actions required under relevant ESRS, because it has not adopted policies and/or actions with reference to the specific sustainability matter concerned, it shall disclose this to be the case, and provide reasons for not having adopted policies and/or actions. The undertaking may disclose a timeframe in which it aims to adopt them.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 62. If the undertaking cannot disclose the information on policies and actions required under relevant ESRS, because it has not adopted policies and/or actions with reference to the specific sustainability matter concerned, it shall disclose this to be the case, and provide reasons for not having adopted policies and/or actions. The undertaking may disclose a timeframe in which it aims to adopt them.

esrs.topicName: Sustainability matter(s) for which targets have not been adopted
esrs.requirementText: 72. If the undertaking cannot disclose the information on targets required under the relevant topical ESRS, because it has not set targets with reference to the specific sustainability matter concerned, it shall disclose this to be the case, and provide reasons for not having adopted targets. The undertaking may disclose a timeframe in which it aims to adopt them.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 72. If the undertaking cannot disclose the information on targets required under the relevant topical ESRS, because it has not set targets with reference to the specific sustainability matter concerned, it shall disclose this to be the case, and provide reasons for not having adopted targets. The undertaking may disclose a timeframe in which it aims to adopt them.

esrs.topicName: Description of process in relation to identification of climate-related transition events, considering at least climate scenario in line with limiting global warming to one and half degrees Celsius with no or limited overshoot [text block]

esrs.disclosureExpectation: the assessment of how its assets and business activities may be exposed to these climate-related transition events, creating gross transition risks or opportunities for the undertaking.

esrs.topicName: Transition events have been identified either over short-, medium- or long-term time horizons
esrs.requirementText: identified transition events (see the table with examples below) over the short-, medium- and long-term and screened whether its assets and business activities may be exposed to these events. In case of transition risks and opportunities, what is considered long-term may cover more than 10 years and may be aligned with climate-related public policy goals;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: identified transition events (see the table with examples below) over the short-, medium- and long-term and screened whether its assets and business activities may be exposed to these events. In case of transition risks and opportunities, what is considered long-term may cover more than 10 years and may be aligned with climate-related public policy goals;

esrs.topicName: Extent to which assets and business activities may be exposed and are sensitive to identified transition events has been assessed
esrs.requirementText: assessed the extent to which its assets and business activities may be exposed and are sensitive to the identified transition events, taking into consideration the likelihood, magnitude and duration of the transition events;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: assessed the extent to which its assets and business activities may be exposed and are sensitive to the identified transition events, taking into consideration the likelihood, magnitude and duration of the transition events;

esrs.topicName: Identification of transition events and (or) assessment of exposure has been informed by climate-related scenario analysis
esrs.requirementText: informed the identification of transition events and the assessment of exposure by climate-related scenario analysis, considering at least a scenario consistent with the Paris Agreement and limiting climate change to 1.5°C, for example, based on scenarios of the International Energy Agency (Net zero Emissions by 2050, Sustainable Development Scenario, etc), or NGFS (Network for Greening the Financial System) climate scenarios. For the general requirements related to climate-related scenario analysis see paragrap...

esrs.topicName: Assets and business activities that are incompatible with or need significant efforts to be compatible with transition to climate-neutral economy have been identified

esrs.disclosureExpectation: identified assets and business activities that are incompatible with or need significant efforts to be compatible with a transition to a climate-neutral economy (for example, due to significant locked-in GHG emissions or incompatibility with the requirements for Taxonomy-alignment under Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/2139).

esrs.topicName: Explanation of how climate-related scenario analysis has been used to inform identification and assessment of transition risks and opportunities over short, medium and long-term [text block]
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 21. When disclosing the information required under paragraphs 20 (b)and 20 (c) the undertaking shall explain how it has used climate-related scenario analysis, including a range of climate scenarios, to inform the identification and assessment of physical risks and transition risks and opportunities over the short-, medium- and long-term.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] 21. When disclosing the information required under paragraphs 20 (b)and 20 (c) the undertaking shall explain how it has used climate-related scenario analysis, including a range of climate scenarios, to inform the identification and assessment of physical risks and transition risks and opportunities over the short-, medium- and long-term.

esrs.topicName: Explanation of extent to which ability to implement action depends on availability and allocation of resources [text block]
esrs.requirementText: AR 21. In line with the requirements of ESRS 2 MDR-A, the undertaking shall explain if and to what extent its ability to implement the actions depends on the availability and allocation of resources. Ongoing access to finance at an affordable cost of capital can be critical for the implementation of the undertaking’s actions, which include its adjustments to supply/demand changes or its related acquisitions and significant research and development (R&D) investments.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of GHG removals and GHG mitigation projects financed through carbon credits [text block]
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 56. The undertaking shall disclose: (a) GHG removals and storage in metric tonnes of CO2eq resulting from projects it may have developed in its own operations, or contributed to in its upstream and downstream value chain; and (b) the amount of GHG emission reductions or removals from climate change mitigation projects outside its value chain it has financed or intends to finance through any purchase of carbon credits. [Related] https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2021/1119/oj

esrs.disclosureExpectation: an explanation of whether the key action plan is carried out only by the undertaking, using the undertaking’s resources, or whether it is part of a wider initiative to which the undertaking significantly contributes. esrs.disclosureExpectation: a description of how it contributes to systemwide change, notably to alter the drivers of biodiversity and ecosystem change, e.g., through technological, economic, institutional, and social factors and changes in underlying values and behaviours;

esrs.topicName: Layer(s) in mitigation hierarchy to which target can be allocated (biodiversity and ecosystems)
esrs.requirementText: to which of the layers of the mitigation hierarchy the target can be allocated (i.e., avoidance, minimisation, restoration and rehabilitation, compensation or offsets).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: to which of the layers of the mitigation hierarchy the target can be allocated (i.e., avoidance, minimisation, restoration and rehabilitation, compensation or offsets).

esrs.topicName: Target addresses shortcomings related to Substantial Contribution criteria for Biodiversity
esrs.requirementText: AR 22. The undertaking may specify whether the target addresses shortcomings related to the Substantial Contribution criteria for Biodiversity as defined in the delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 15(2) of Regulation (EU) 2020/852. Where the Do No Significant Harm (DNSH) criteria for Biodiversity as defined in delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 10(3), Article 11(3), Article 12(2), Article 13(2), and Article 14(2) of Regulation (EU) 2020/852 are not met, the undertaking may specify whether the targ...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 22. The undertaking may specify whether the target addresses shortcomings related to the Substantial Contribution criteria for Biodiversity as defined in the delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 15(2) of Regulation (EU) 2020/852. Where the Do No Significant Harm (DNSH) criteria for Biodiversity as defined in delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 10(3), Article 11(3), Article 12(2), Article 13(2), and Article 14(2) of Regulation (EU) 2020/852 are not met, the undertaking may specify whether the targ...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics related to material impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 33. The undertaking shall report metrics related to its material impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems .
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 33. The undertaking shall report metrics related to its material impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems .

esrs.topicName: Number of sites owned, leased or managed in or near protected areas or key biodiversity areas that undertaking is negatively affecting
esrs.requirementText: 35. If the undertaking identified sites located in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas that it is negatively affecting (see paragraph 19(a)), the undertaking shall disclose the number and area (in hectares) of sites owned, leased or managed in or near these protected areas or key biodiversity areas.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 35. If the undertaking identified sites located in or near biodiversity-sensitive areas that it is negatively affecting (see paragraph 19(a)), the undertaking shall disclose the number and area (in hectares) of sites owned, leased or managed in or near these protected areas or key biodiversity areas.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of land-use based on Life Cycle Assessment [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 36. If the undertaking has identified material impacts with regards to land-use change, or impacts on the extent and condition of ecosystems , it may also disclose their land-use based on a Life Cycle Assessment.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 36. If the undertaking has identified material impacts with regards to land-use change, or impacts on the extent and condition of ecosystems , it may also disclose their land-use based on a Life Cycle Assessment.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics considered relevant for land-use change, freshwater-use change and (or) sea-use change [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 38. If the undertaking has concluded that it directly contributes to the impact drivers of land-use change , freshwater -use change and/or sea-use change, it shall report relevant metrics. The undertaking may disclose metrics that measure: (a) the conversion over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) of land cover (e.g. deforestation or mining); (b) changes over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) in the management of the ecosystem (e.g., through the intensification of agricultural management, or the application of better management pr...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 38. If the undertaking has concluded that it directly contributes to the impact drivers of land-use change , freshwater -use change and/or sea-use change, it shall report relevant metrics. The undertaking may disclose metrics that measure: (a) the conversion over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) of land cover (e.g. deforestation or mining); (b) changes over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) in the management of the ecosystem (e.g., through the intensification of agricultural management, or the application of better management pr...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure conversion over time of land cover [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the conversion over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) of land cover (e.g. deforestation or mining);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the conversion over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) of land cover (e.g. deforestation or mining);

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes over time in management of ecosystem [text block]
esrs.requirementText: changes over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) in the management of the ecosystem (e.g., through the intensification of agricultural management, or the application of better management practices or forestry harvesting);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: changes over time (e.g. 1 or 5 years) in the management of the ecosystem (e.g., through the intensification of agricultural management, or the application of better management practices or forestry harvesting);

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes in spatial configuration of landscape [text block]
esrs.requirementText: changes in the spatial configuration of the landscape (e.g. fragmentation of habitats, changes in ecosystem connectivity);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: changes in the spatial configuration of the landscape (e.g. fragmentation of habitats, changes in ecosystem connectivity);

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes in ecosystem structural connectivity [text block]
esrs.requirementText: changes in ecosystem structural connectivity (e.g. habitat permeability based on physical features and arrangements of habitat patches); and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: changes in ecosystem structural connectivity (e.g. habitat permeability based on physical features and arrangements of habitat patches); and

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure functional connectivity [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the functional connectivity (e.g. how well genes or individuals move through land, freshwater and seascape).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the functional connectivity (e.g. how well genes or individuals move through land, freshwater and seascape).

esrs.topicName: Total use of land area
esrs.requirementText: (a) total use of land;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: (a) total use of land;

esrs.topicName: Total sealed area
esrs.requirementText: (b) total sealed area;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: (b) total sealed area;

esrs.topicName: Total nature-oriented area on site
esrs.requirementText: total nature-oriented area on site ; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: total nature-oriented area on site ; and

esrs.topicName: Total nature-oriented area off site
esrs.requirementText: total nature-oriented area off site.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: total nature-oriented area off site.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of pathways of introduction and spread of invasive alien species [text block]
esrs.requirementText: AR 32. With regard to the introduction of invasive alien species, the undertaking may disclose the pathways and number of invasive alien species and the extent of surface covered by invasive alien species.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 32. With regard to the introduction of invasive alien species, the undertaking may disclose the pathways and number of invasive alien species and the extent of surface covered by invasive alien species.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics used to manage pathways of introduction and spread of invasive alien species and risks posed by invasive alien species [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 39. If the undertaking concluded that it directly contributes to the accidental or voluntary introduction of invasive alien species, the undertaking may disclose the metrics it uses to manage pathways of introduction and spread of invasive alien species and the risks posed by invasive alien species.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 39. If the undertaking concluded that it directly contributes to the accidental or voluntary introduction of invasive alien species, the undertaking may disclose the metrics it uses to manage pathways of introduction and spread of invasive alien species and the risks posed by invasive alien species.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics considered relevant for state of species [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 40. If the undertaking identified material impacts related to the state of species, the undertaking may report metrics it considers relevant. The undertaking may: (a) refer to relevant disclosure requirements in ESRS E1, ESRS E2, ESRS E3, and ESRS E5; (b) consider population size, range within specific ecosystems as well as extinction risk. These aspects provide insight on the health of a single species’ population and its relative resilience to human induced and naturally occurring change; (c) disclose metrics...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 40. If the undertaking identified material impacts related to the state of species, the undertaking may report metrics it considers relevant. The undertaking may: (a) refer to relevant disclosure requirements in ESRS E1, ESRS E2, ESRS E3, and ESRS E5; (b) consider population size, range within specific ecosystems as well as extinction risk. These aspects provide insight on the health of a single species’ population and its relative resilience to human induced and naturally occurring change; (c) disclose metrics...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of population size, range within specific ecosystems and extinction risk [text block]
esrs.requirementText: consider population size, range within specific ecosystems as well as extinction risk. These aspects provide insight on the health of a single species’ population and its relative resilience to human induced and naturally occurring change;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: consider population size, range within specific ecosystems as well as extinction risk. These aspects provide insight on the health of a single species’ population and its relative resilience to human induced and naturally occurring change;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes in number of individuals of species within specific area [text block]
esrs.requirementText: disclose metrics that measure changes in the number of individuals of a species within a specific area;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: disclose metrics that measure changes in the number of individuals of a species within a specific area;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics on species at extinction risk [text block]
esrs.requirementText: disclose metrics on species at extinction risk ( 87 ) that measure:
esrs.disclosureExpectation: disclose metrics on species at extinction risk ( 87 ) that measure:

esrs.requirementText: the threat status of species and how activities/pressures may affect the threat status; or
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the threat status of species and how activities/pressures may affect the threat status; or

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes in relevant habitat for threatened species as proxy for impact on local population's extinction risk [text block]
esrs.requirementText: changes in the relevant habitat for a threatened species as a proxy for the undertaking’s impact on the local population’s extinction risk.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: changes in the relevant habitat for a threatened species as a proxy for the undertaking’s impact on the local population’s extinction risk.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics considered relevant for extent and condition of ecosystems [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 41. If the undertaking identified material impacts related to ecosystems, it may disclose: (a) with regard to ecosystems extent, metrics that measure area coverage of a particular ecosystem without necessarily considering the quality of the area being assessed, such as habitat cover. For example, forest cover is a measure of the extent of a particular ecosystem type, without factoring in the condition of the ecosystem (e.g., provides the area without describing the species diversity within the forest). (b) with...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 41. If the undertaking identified material impacts related to ecosystems, it may disclose: (a) with regard to ecosystems extent, metrics that measure area coverage of a particular ecosystem without necessarily considering the quality of the area being assessed, such as habitat cover. For example, forest cover is a measure of the extent of a particular ecosystem type, without factoring in the condition of the ecosystem (e.g., provides the area without describing the species diversity within the forest). (b) with...

esrs.topicName: Description of methodologies and metrics used and explanation of why methodologies and metrics are selected, their assumptions, limitations and uncertainties, and any changes in methodologies made over time and why they occurred [text block]
esrs.requirementText: [Main] the methodologies and metrics used and an explanation for why these methodologies and metrics are selected, as well as their assumptions, limitations and uncertainties, and any changes in methodologies made over time and why they occurred; [Main] whether metrics are mandatory (required by legislation) or voluntary. If they are mandatory, the undertaking may indicate the relevant legislation; if voluntary, the undertaking may refer to any voluntary standard or procedure used; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] the methodologies and metrics used and an explanation for why these methodologies and metrics are selected, as well as their assumptions, limitations and uncertainties, and any changes in methodologies made over time and why they occurred; [Main] whether metrics are mandatory (required by legislation) or voluntary. If they are mandatory, the undertaking may indicate the relevant legislation; if voluntary, the undertaking may refer to any voluntary standard or procedure used; and

esrs.topicName: Description of geographies covered by methodology and explanation of why any relevant geographies were omitted [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the geographies covered by the methodology and an explanation of why any relevant geographies were omitted;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the geographies covered by the methodology and an explanation of why any relevant geographies were omitted;

esrs.topicName: Description of metric(s) scope [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the scope of the metrics and methodologies, for example:
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the scope of the metrics and methodologies, for example:

esrs.topicName: Biodiversity component(s) of metric(s)
esrs.requirementText: the biodiversity components of the metrics: species specific, ecosystem specific;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the biodiversity components of the metrics: species specific, ecosystem specific;

esrs.topicName: Description of how metric(s) related to biodiversity and ecosystems change integrate ecological thresholds and allocations [text block]
esrs.requirementText: how the metrics integrate ecological thresholds (e.g., the biosphere integrity and land-system change, planetary boundaries ) and allocations;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: how the metrics integrate ecological thresholds (e.g., the biosphere integrity and land-system change, planetary boundaries ) and allocations;

esrs.topicName: Description of frequency of monitoring of metric(s) related to biodiversity and ecosystems change [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the frequency of monitoring, key metrics being monitored, and the baseline condition/value and baseline year/period, as well as the reference period;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the frequency of monitoring, key metrics being monitored, and the baseline condition/value and baseline year/period, as well as the reference period;

esrs.topicName: Data source(s) (biodiversity and ecosystems change)
esrs.requirementText: whether these metrics rely on primary data, secondary data, modelled data or on expert judgement, or a mixture of these;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: whether these metrics rely on primary data, secondary data, modelled data or on expert judgement, or a mixture of these;

esrs.topicName: Identifier(s) of related action (plans)
esrs.requirementText: [Main] it shall disclose whether it nevertheless tracks the effectiveness of its policies and actions in relation to the material sustainability-related impact, risk and opportunity , and if so: [Main] an indication of which action is measured and monitored via the metrics and how they relate to the achievement of targets ;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] it shall disclose whether it nevertheless tracks the effectiveness of its policies and actions in relation to the material sustainability-related impact, risk and opportunity , and if so: [Main] an indication of which action is measured and monitored via the metrics and how they relate to the achievement of targets ;

esrs.topicName: Metric(s) are mandatory (required by legislation)
esrs.requirementText: whether metrics are mandatory (required by legislation) or voluntary. If they are mandatory, the undertaking may indicate the relevant legislation; if voluntary, the undertaking may refer to any voluntary standard or procedure used; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: whether metrics are mandatory (required by legislation) or voluntary. If they are mandatory, the undertaking may indicate the relevant legislation; if voluntary, the undertaking may refer to any voluntary standard or procedure used; and

esrs.topicName: Metric(s) are informed by or correspond to expectations or recommendations of relevant and authoritative national, EU-level or intergovernmental guidelines, policies, legislation or agreements
esrs.requirementText: whether the metrics are informed by or correspond to expectations or recommendations of relevant and authoritative national, EU-level or intergovernmental guidelines, policies , legislation or agreements, such as the Convention for Biological Diversity (CBD) or IPBES.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: whether the metrics are informed by or correspond to expectations or recommendations of relevant and authoritative national, EU-level or intergovernmental guidelines, policies , legislation or agreements, such as the Convention for Biological Diversity (CBD) or IPBES.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of paragraph in other ESRS environment-related standard in which state of species metric is referred to [text block]
esrs.requirementText: refer to relevant disclosure requirements in ESRS E1, ESRS E2, ESRS E3, and ESRS E5;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: refer to relevant disclosure requirements in ESRS E1, ESRS E2, ESRS E3, and ESRS E5;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure area coverage of ecosystem [text block]
esrs.requirementText: with regard to ecosystems extent, metrics that measure area coverage of a particular ecosystem without necessarily considering the quality of the area being assessed, such as habitat cover. For example, forest cover is a measure of the extent of a particular ecosystem type, without factoring in the condition of the ecosystem (e.g., provides the area without describing the species diversity within the forest).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: with regard to ecosystems extent, metrics that measure area coverage of a particular ecosystem without necessarily considering the quality of the area being assessed, such as habitat cover. For example, forest cover is a measure of the extent of a particular ecosystem type, without factoring in the condition of the ecosystem (e.g., provides the area without describing the species diversity within the forest).

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure quality of ecosystem relative to pre-determined reference state [text block]
esrs.requirementText: metrics that measure the quality of ecosystems relative to a pre-determined reference state;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: metrics that measure the quality of ecosystems relative to a pre-determined reference state;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure multiple species within ecosystem [text block]
esrs.requirementText: metrics that measure multiple species within an ecosystem rather than the number of individuals within a single species within an ecosystem (for example: scientifically established species richness and abundance indicators that measure the development of (native) species composition within an ecosystem against the reference state at the beginning of the first reporting period as well as the targeted state outlined in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, or an aggregation of species’ conservation s...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: metrics that measure multiple species within an ecosystem rather than the number of individuals within a single species within an ecosystem (for example: scientifically established species richness and abundance indicators that measure the development of (native) species composition within an ecosystem against the reference state at the beginning of the first reporting period as well as the targeted state outlined in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, or an aggregation of species’ conservation s...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure structural components of ecosystem condition [text block]
esrs.requirementText: metrics that reflect structural components of condition such as habitat connectivity (i.e., how linked habitats are to each other).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: metrics that reflect structural components of condition such as habitat connectivity (i.e., how linked habitats are to each other).

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure process or function that ecosystem completes, or that reflect ability of ecosystem to undertake that specific process or function [text block]
esrs.requirementText: a metric that measures a process or function that the ecosystem completes, or that reflects the ability of the ecosystem to undertake that specific process or function: e.g. net primary productivity, which is a measure of plant productivity based on the rate at which energy is stored by plants and made available to other species in the ecosystem. It is a core process that occurs for ecosystems to function. It is related to many factors, such as species diversity, but does not measure these factors directly; or
esrs.disclosureExpectation: a metric that measures a process or function that the ecosystem completes, or that reflects the ability of the ecosystem to undertake that specific process or function: e.g. net primary productivity, which is a measure of plant productivity based on the rate at which energy is stored by plants and made available to other species in the ecosystem. It is a core process that occurs for ecosystems to function. It is related to many factors, such as species diversity, but does not measure these factors directly; or

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of metrics that measure changes to population of scientifically identified species under threat [text block]
esrs.requirementText: A metric that measures changes to the population of scientifically identified species under threat.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: A metric that measures changes to the population of scientifically identified species under threat.

esrs.topicName: Information about methodologies, assumptions and tools used to screen assets and (or) activities in order to identify actual and potential impacts, risks and opportunities in either own operations or value chain [text block]
esrs.requirementText: whether the undertaking has screened its assets and activities in order to identify its actual and potential impacts, risks and opportunities in its own operations and its upstream and downstream value chain, and if so, the methodologies, assumptions and tools used in the screening;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: whether the undertaking has screened its assets and activities in order to identify its actual and potential impacts, risks and opportunities in its own operations and its upstream and downstream value chain, and if so, the methodologies, assumptions and tools used in the screening;

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of policies adopted to manage material impacts, risks and opportunities related to resource use and circular economy [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 12. The undertaking shall describe its policies adopted to manage its material impacts, risks and opportunities related to resource use and circular economy.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 12. The undertaking shall describe its policies adopted to manage its material impacts, risks and opportunities related to resource use and circular economy.

esrs.topicName: Policy addresses transitioning away from use of virgin resources
esrs.requirementText: transitioning away from use of virgin resources, including relative increases in use of secondary (recycled) resources;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: transitioning away from use of virgin resources, including relative increases in use of secondary (recycled) resources;

esrs.topicName: Policy addresses sustainable sourcing and (or) use of renewable resources
esrs.requirementText: sustainable sourcing and use of renewable resources.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: sustainable sourcing and use of renewable resources.

esrs.topicName: Layer(s) in waste hierarchy that policy addresses
esrs.requirementText: the waste hierarchy : (a) prevention; (b) preparing for re-use; (c) recycling ; (d) other recovery , e.g., energy recovery; and (e) disposal. In this context, the waste treatment cannot be considered as a recovery;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the waste hierarchy : (a) prevention; (b) preparing for re-use; (c) recycling ; (d) other recovery , e.g., energy recovery; and (e) disposal. In this context, the waste treatment cannot be considered as a recovery;

esrs.topicName: Policy addresses prioritisation of avoidance or minimisation waste (Re-use, Repair, Refurbish, Remanufacture and Repurpose) over waste treatment (Recycling)
esrs.requirementText: the prioritisation of the avoidance or minimisation waste (Re-use, Repair, Refurbish, Remanufacture and Repurpose) over waste treatment (Recycling). The concepts of eco-design ( 93 ) , waste as a resource or post- consumer waste (at the end of a consumer -product lifecycle), should also be taken into consideration.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the prioritisation of the avoidance or minimisation waste (Re-use, Repair, Refurbish, Remanufacture and Repurpose) over waste treatment (Recycling). The concepts of eco-design ( 93 ) , waste as a resource or post- consumer waste (at the end of a consumer -product lifecycle), should also be taken into consideration.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of resource use and circular economy actions and resources allocated to their implementation [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 17. The undertaking shall disclose its resource use and circular economy actions and the resources allocated to their implementation.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 17. The undertaking shall disclose its resource use and circular economy actions and the resources allocated to their implementation.

esrs.topicName: Action covers higher levels of resource efficiency in use of technical and biological materials and (or) water
esrs.requirementText: higher levels of resource efficiency in use of technical and biological materials and water, particularly in relation to critical raw materials and rare earths as listed in the Raw Materials Information System;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: higher levels of resource efficiency in use of technical and biological materials and water, particularly in relation to critical raw materials and rare earths as listed in the Raw Materials Information System;

esrs.topicName: Action covers higher rates of use of secondary raw materials (recyclates)
esrs.requirementText: higher rates of use of secondary raw materials (recyclates);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: higher rates of use of secondary raw materials (recyclates);

esrs.topicName: Action covers application of circular design and (or) higher rates of either Reuse, Repair, Refurbishing, Remanufacture, Repurposing or Recycling
esrs.requirementText: application of circular design, leading to increased product durability and optimisation of use, and higher rates of: Reuse, Repair, Refurbishing, Remanufacture, Repurposing and Recycling.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: application of circular design, leading to increased product durability and optimisation of use, and higher rates of: Reuse, Repair, Refurbishing, Remanufacture, Repurposing and Recycling.

esrs.topicName: Action covers application of circular business practices
esrs.requirementText: application of circular business practices such as (i) value retention actions (maintenance, repair, refurbishing, remanufacturing, component harvesting, upgrading and reverse logistics, closed loop systems, second-hand retailing), (ii) value maximisation actions (product-service systems, collaborative and sharing economy business models), (iii) end-of-life actions ( recycling , upcycling, extended producer responsibility), and (iv) systems efficiency actions (industrial symbiosis);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: application of circular business practices such as (i) value retention actions (maintenance, repair, refurbishing, remanufacturing, component harvesting, upgrading and reverse logistics, closed loop systems, second-hand retailing), (ii) value maximisation actions (product-service systems, collaborative and sharing economy business models), (iii) end-of-life actions ( recycling , upcycling, extended producer responsibility), and (iv) systems efficiency actions (industrial symbiosis);

esrs.topicName: Action covers actions taken to prevent waste generation in upstream and downstream value chain
esrs.requirementText: actions taken to prevent waste generation in the undertaking’s upstream and downstream value chain; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: actions taken to prevent waste generation in the undertaking’s upstream and downstream value chain; and

esrs.topicName: Action covers optimisation of waste management in line with waste hierarchy
esrs.requirementText: optimistation of waste management in line with the waste hierarchy .
esrs.disclosureExpectation: optimistation of waste management in line with the waste hierarchy .

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of resource use and circular economy-related targets [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 21. The undertaking shall disclose the resource use and circular economy-related targets it has set.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 21. The undertaking shall disclose the resource use and circular economy-related targets it has set.

esrs.topicName: Target relates to increase of circular product design
esrs.requirementText: the increase of circular product design (including for instance design for durability , dismantling, reparability, recyclability etc);
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the increase of circular product design (including for instance design for durability , dismantling, reparability, recyclability etc);

esrs.topicName: Target relates to increase of circular material use rate
esrs.requirementText: the increase of circular material use rate ;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the increase of circular material use rate ;

esrs.requirementText: the minimisation of primary raw material;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the minimisation of primary raw material;

esrs.topicName: Target relates to sustainable sourcing and (or) use of renewable resources
esrs.requirementText: sustainable sourcing and use (in line with the cascading principle) of renewable resources;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: sustainable sourcing and use (in line with the cascading principle) of renewable resources;

esrs.topicName: Layer(s) in waste hierarchy to which target relates
esrs.requirementText: 25. The undertaking shall specify to which layer of the waste hierarchy the target relates.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 25. The undertaking shall specify to which layer of the waste hierarchy the target relates.

Where the Do No Significant Harm (DNSH) criteria for Circular Economy as defined in delegated acts adopted pursuant to Article 10(3), Article 11(3), Article 12(2), Article 14(2), and Article 15(2) of Regulation (EU) 2020/852 are not met, the undertaking may specify whether...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of information on resource inflows related to material impacts, risks and opportunities [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 28. The undertaking shall disclose information on its resource inflows related to its material impacts, risks and opportunities.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 28. The undertaking shall disclose information on its resource inflows related to its material impacts, risks and opportunities.

esrs.topicName: Description of resource inflows for products and materials, water and property, plant and equipment used in own operations and along upstream value chain [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 30. The disclosure required by paragraph 28 shall include a description of its resource inflows where material: products (including packaging ) and materials (specifying critical raw materials and rare earths), water and property, plant and equipment used in the undertaking’s own operations and along its upstream value chain.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 30. The disclosure required by paragraph 28 shall include a description of its resource inflows where material: products (including packaging ) and materials (specifying critical raw materials and rare earths), water and property, plant and equipment used in the undertaking’s own operations and along its upstream value chain.

esrs.topicName: Information about materials used to manufacture products and services [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 31. When an undertaking assesses that resource inflows is a material sustainability matter, it shall disclose the following information about the materials used to manufacture the undertaking’s products and services during the reporting period, in tonnes or kilogrammes: (a) the overall total weight of products and technical and biological materials used during the reporting period; (b) the percentage of biological materials (and biofuels used for non-energy purposes) used to manufacture the undertaking’s product...
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 31. When an undertaking assesses that resource inflows is a material sustainability matter, it shall disclose the following information about the materials used to manufacture the undertaking’s products and services during the reporting period, in tonnes or kilogrammes: (a) the overall total weight of products and technical and biological materials used during the reporting period; (b) the percentage of biological materials (and biofuels used for non-energy purposes) used to manufacture the undertaking’s product...

esrs.topicName: Total products and technical and biological materials used
esrs.requirementText: the overall total weight of products and technical and biological materials used during the reporting period;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the overall total weight of products and technical and biological materials used during the reporting period;

esrs.topicName: Percentage of biological materials used to manufacture products and services that are sustainably sourced
esrs.requirementText: the percentage of biological materials (and biofuels used for non-energy purposes) used to manufacture the undertaking’s products and services (including packaging ) that is sustainably sourced, with the information on the certification scheme used and on the application of the cascading principle; and

esrs.requirementText: the weight in both absolute value and percentage, of secondary reused or recycled components, secondary intermediary products and secondary materials used to manufacture the undertaking’s products and services (including packaging).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the weight in both absolute value and percentage, of secondary reused or recycled components, secondary intermediary products and secondary materials used to manufacture the undertaking’s products and services (including packaging).

esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 25. In cases where there is an overlap between categories of reused, recycled, the undertaking shall specify how double counting was avoided and the choices that were made.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of information on resource outflows related to material impacts, risks and opportunities [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 33. The undertaking shall disclose information on its resource outflows, including waste, related to its material impacts, risks and opportunities.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 33. The undertaking shall disclose information on its resource outflows, including waste, related to its material impacts, risks and opportunities.

esrs.topicName: Description of key products and materials that come out of production process and that are designed along circular principles [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 35. The undertaking shall provide a description of the key products and materials that come out of the undertaking’s production process and that are designed along circular principles, including durability , reusability, repairability, disassembly, remanufacturing, refurbishment, recycling , recirculation by the biological cycle, or optimisation of the use of the product or material through other circular business models.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 35. The undertaking shall provide a description of the key products and materials that come out of the undertaking’s production process and that are designed along circular principles, including durability , reusability, repairability, disassembly, remanufacturing, refurbishment, recycling , recirculation by the biological cycle, or optimisation of the use of the product or material through other circular business models.

esrs.topicName: Total waste generated
esrs.requirementText: the total amount of waste generated ;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the total amount of waste generated ;

esrs.topicName: Total waste generated diverted from disposal
esrs.requirementText: the total amount by weight diverted from disposal, with a breakdown between hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste and a breakdown by the following recovery operation types:
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the total amount by weight diverted from disposal, with a breakdown between hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste and a breakdown by the following recovery operation types:

esrs.topicName: Total waste generated directed to disposal
esrs.requirementText: the amount by weight directed to disposal by waste treatment type and the total amount summing all three types, with a breakdown between hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste. The waste treatment types to be disclosed are:
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the amount by weight directed to disposal by waste treatment type and the total amount summing all three types, with a breakdown between hazardous waste and non-hazardous waste. The waste treatment types to be disclosed are:

esrs.topicName: Total non-recycled waste
esrs.requirementText: [Main] the total amount and percentage of non-recycled waste ( 91 ) . [Related] https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2022/1288/oj
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] the total amount and percentage of non-recycled waste ( 91 ) . [Related] https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2022/1288/oj

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of composition of waste [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 38. When disclosing the composition of the waste , the undertaking shall specify: (a) the waste streams relevant to its sector or activities (e.g. tailings for the undertaking in the mining sector, electronic waste for the undertaking in the consumer electronics sector, or food waste for the undertaking in the agriculture or in the hospitality sector); and; (b) the materials that are present in the waste (e.g. biomass, metals, non-metallic minerals, plastics, textiles, critical raw materials and rare earths).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 38. When disclosing the composition of the waste , the undertaking shall specify: (a) the waste streams relevant to its sector or activities (e.g. tailings for the undertaking in the mining sector, electronic waste for the undertaking in the consumer electronics sector, or food waste for the undertaking in the agriculture or in the hospitality sector); and; (b) the materials that are present in the waste (e.g. biomass, metals, non-metallic minerals, plastics, textiles, critical raw materials and rare earths).

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of engagement in product end-of-life waste management [text block]
esrs.requirementText: AR 28. The undertaking may disclose its engagement in product end-of-life waste management , for example through extended producer responsibility schemes or take-back schemes.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 28. The undertaking may disclose its engagement in product end-of-life waste management , for example through extended producer responsibility schemes or take-back schemes.

esrs.clauseId: E5 E5-5 39 Indicator 9 Hazardous waste and radioactive waste ratio
esrs.topicName: Total hazardous waste
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 39. The undertaking shall also disclose the total amount of hazardous waste and radioactive waste generated by the undertaking, where radioactive waste is defined in Article 3(7) of Council Directive 2011/70/Euratom ( 92 ) . [Related] https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2022/1288/oj
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] 39. If the undertaking cannot report exact figures, it shall use estimates according to the provisions in ESRS 1 to disclose the number of people in its own workforce who are not employees to the nearest ten or, where the number of people in its own workforce who are not employees is gr...

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of extent to which working conditions and terms of employment of employees are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements and extent to which employees are represented in social dialogue in European Economic Area (EEA) [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 58. The undertaking shall disclose information on the extent to which the working conditions and terms of employment of its employees are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements and on the extent to which its employees are represented in social dialogue in the European Economic Area (EEA).
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 58. The undertaking shall disclose information on the extent to which the working conditions and terms of employment of its employees are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements and on the extent to which its employees are represented in social dialogue in the European Economic Area (EEA).

esrs.topicName: Percentage of total employees covered by collective bargaining agreements
esrs.requirementText: the percentage of its total employees covered by collective bargaining agreements;
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the percentage of its total employees covered by collective bargaining agreements;

esrs.topicName: Undertaking has one or more collective bargaining agreements (EEA)
esrs.requirementText: in the EEA, whether it has one or more collective bargaining agreements and, if so, the overall percentage of its employees covered by such agreement(s) for each country in which it has significant employment, defined as at least 50 employees by head count representing at least 10% of its total number of employees; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: in the EEA, whether it has one or more collective bargaining agreements and, if so, the overall percentage of its employees covered by such agreement(s) for each country in which it has significant employment, defined as at least 50 employees by head count representing at least 10% of its total number of employees; and

esrs.topicName: Basis for determination of working conditions and terms of employment for employees not covered by collective bargaining agreements
esrs.requirementText: 61. For employees not covered by collective bargaining agreements, the undertaking may disclose whether it determines their working conditions and terms of employment based on collective bargaining agreements that cover its other employees or based on collective bargaining agreements from other undertakings.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 61. For employees not covered by collective bargaining agreements, the undertaking may disclose whether it determines their working conditions and terms of employment based on collective bargaining agreements that cover its other employees or based on collective bargaining agreements from other undertakings.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of extent to which working conditions and terms of employment of non-employees in own workforce are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 62. The undertaking may disclose the extent to which the working conditions and terms of employment of non-employees in its own workforce are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements, including an estimate of the coverage rate.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 62. The undertaking may disclose the extent to which the working conditions and terms of employment of non-employees in its own workforce are determined or influenced by collective bargaining agreements, including an estimate of the coverage rate.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of existence of any agreement with employees for representation by European Works Council (EWC), Societas Europaea (SE) Works Council, or Societas Cooperativa Europaea (SCE) Works Council [text block]
esrs.requirementText: the existence of any agreement with its employees for representation by a European Works Council (EWC), a Societas Europaea (SE) Works Council, or a Societas Cooperativa Europaea (SCE) Works Council.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the existence of any agreement with its employees for representation by a European Works Council (EWC), a Societas Europaea (SE) Works Council, or a Societas Cooperativa Europaea (SCE) Works Council.

esrs.topicName: Coverage rate of employees in country (EEA) that are covered at establishment level by workers' representatives
esrs.requirementText: the global percentage of employees covered by workers’ representatives, reported at the country level for each EEA country in which the undertaking has significant employment; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the global percentage of employees covered by workers’ representatives, reported at the country level for each EEA country in which the undertaking has significant employment; and

esrs.topicName: Coverage rate of employees in region (non-EEA) that are covered by collective bargaining agreements
esrs.requirementText: outside the EEA, the percentage of its own employees covered by collective bargaining agreements by region.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: outside the EEA, the percentage of its own employees covered by collective bargaining agreements by region.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of gender distribution at top management and age distribution amongst employees [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 64. The undertaking shall disclose the gender distribution at top management and the age distribution amongst its employees.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 64. The undertaking shall disclose the gender distribution at top management and the age distribution amongst its employees.

esrs.topicName: Number of employees (head count) under 30 years old, during period
esrs.requirementText: the distribution of employees by age group: under 30 years old; 30-50 years old; over 50 years old.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the distribution of employees by age group: under 30 years old; 30-50 years old; over 50 years old.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of own definition of top management used [text block]
esrs.requirementText: AR 71. In preparing the disclosure on gender at top management, the undertaking shall use the definition of top management as one and two levels below the administrative and supervisory bodies unless this concept has already been defined with the undertaking’s operations and differs from the previous description. If this is the case, the undertaking can use its own definition for top management and disclose that fact and its own definition.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 71. In preparing the disclosure on gender at top management, the undertaking shall use the definition of top management as one and two levels below the administrative and supervisory bodies unless this concept has already been defined with the undertaking’s operations and differs from the previous description. If this is the case, the undertaking can use its own definition for top management and disclose that fact and its own definition.

esrs.topicName: Number of employees (head count) at top management level, during period
esrs.requirementText: the gender distribution in number and percentage at top management level; and
esrs.disclosureExpectation: the gender distribution in number and percentage at top management level; and

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of whether or not employees are paid adequate wage, and if not all employees are paid adequate wage, countries and percentage of employees concerned [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 67. The undertaking shall disclose whether or not its employees are paid an adequate wage, and if they are not all paid an adequate wage, the countries and percentage of employees concerned.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 67. The undertaking shall disclose whether or not its employees are paid an adequate wage, and if they are not all paid an adequate wage, the countries and percentage of employees concerned.

esrs.topicName: All employees and (or) non-employees are paid adequate wage in line with applicable benchmarks
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 69. The undertaking shall disclose whether all its employees are paid an adequate wage , in line with applicable benchmarks. If so, stating this will be sufficient to fulfil this disclosure requirement and no further information is needed. [Related] 71. The undertaking may also disclose the information specified in this disclosure requirement with regard to non-employees in its workforce.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: [Main] 69. The undertaking shall disclose whether all its employees are paid an adequate wage , in line with applicable benchmarks. If so, stating this will be sufficient to fulfil this disclosure requirement and no further information is needed. [Related] 71. esrs.topicName: Countries where employees and (or) non-employees earn below applicable adequate wage benchmark
esrs.requirementText: [Main] 70. If not all its employees are paid an adequate wage in line with applicable benchmarks, the undertaking shall disclose the countries where employees earn below the applicable adequate wage benchmark and the percentage of employees that earn below the applicable adequate wage benchmark for each of these countries. [Related] 71. The undertaking may also disclose the information specified in this disclosure requirement with regard to non-employees in its workforce.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of whether employees are covered by social protection against loss of income due to major life events and countries where this is not case [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 72. The undertaking shall disclose whether its employees are covered by social protection against loss of income due to major life events, and, if not, the countries where this is not the case.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 72. The undertaking shall disclose whether its employees are covered by social protection against loss of income due to major life events, and, if not, the countries where this is not the case.

esrs.topicName: Employees and (or) non-employees coverage by social protection, through public programs or through benefits offered, against loss of income due to major life events
esrs.requirementText: 74. The undertaking shall disclose whether all its employees are covered by social protection , through public programs or through benefits offered by the undertaking, against loss of income due to any of the following major life events: (a) sickness; (b) unemployment starting from when the own worker is working for the undertaking; (c) employment injury and acquired disability; (d) parental leave; and (e) retirement. If so, stating this is sufficient to fulfil this disclosure requirement and no further informat...

esrs.requirementText: 75. If not all of its employees are covered by social protection in accordance with paragraph 72, the undertaking shall in addition disclose the countries where employees do not have social protection with regard to one or more of the types of events listed in paragraph 72 and for each of those countries the types of employees who do not have social protection with regard to each applicable major life event.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 75. esrs.requirementText: [Main] the percentage of employees that participated in regular performance and career development reviews; such information shall be broken down by gender; [Related] 85. The undertaking may also disclose the information specified in this disclosure requirement with regard to non-employees in its workforce.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of extent to which own workforce is covered by health and safety management system [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 86. The undertaking shall disclose information on the extent to which its own workforce is covered by its health and safety management system and the number of incidents associated with work-related injuries, ill health and fatalities of its own workforce. In addition, it shall disclose the number of fatalities as a result of work-related injuries and work-related ill health of other workers working on the undertaking’s sites.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 86. The undertaking shall disclose information on the extent to which its own workforce is covered by its health and safety management system and the number of incidents associated with work-related injuries, ill health and fatalities of its own workforce. In addition, it shall disclose the number of fatalities as a result of work-related injuries and work-related ill health of other workers working on the undertaking’s sites.

esrs.topicName: Health and safety management system, or certain parts thereof, has been subject to internal audit or external certification
esrs.requirementText: AR 81. With regard to paragraph 90, when the undertaking’s health and safety management system, or certain parts thereof, has been subject to an internal audit or external certification, the undertaking may state this fact, or absence thereof, and the underlying standards for such audits/certifications, as applicable.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 81. With regard to paragraph 90, when the undertaking’s health and safety management system, or certain parts thereof, has been subject to an internal audit or external certification, the undertaking may state this fact, or absence thereof, and the underlying standards for such audits/certifications, as applicable.

esrs.topicName: Percentage of own workers covered by health and safety management system based on legal requirements and (or) recognised standards or guidelines and which has been internally audited and (or) audited or certified by external party
esrs.requirementText: 90. In addition, the undertaking may include the following additional information on the health and safety coverage: the percentage of its own workers covered by a health and safety management system which is based on legal requirements and/or recognised standards or guidelines and which has been internally audited and/or audited or certified by an external party.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 90. In addition, the undertaking may include the following additional information on the health and safety coverage: the percentage of its own workers covered by a health and safety management system which is based on legal requirements and/or recognised standards or guidelines and which has been internally audited and/or audited or certified by an external party.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of number of work-related incidents and (or) complaints and severe human rights impacts within own workforce and any related fines, sanctions or compensation [text block]
esrs.requirementText: 100. The undertaking shall disclose the number of work-related incidents and/or complaints and severe human rights impacts within its own workforce, and any related fines, sanctions or compensation for the reporting period.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: 100. The undertaking shall disclose the number of work-related incidents and/or complaints and severe human rights impacts within its own workforce, and any related fines, sanctions or compensation for the reporting period.

esrs.topicName: Disclosure of status of incidents and (or) complaints and actions taken (incidents, complaints and severe human rights impacts) [text block]
esrs.requirementText: AR 103. In addition to the information required by paragraphs 103 and 104, the undertaking may disclose the status of incidents and/or complaints and actions taken with reference to the following: (a) incidents reviewed by the undertaking; (b) remediation plans being implemented; (c) remediation plans that have been implemented, with results reviewed through routine internal management review processes; and (d) incidents no longer subject to action.
esrs.disclosureExpectation: AR 103. In addition to the information required by paragraphs 103 and 104, the undertaking may disclose the status of incidents and/or complaints and actions taken with reference to the following: (a) incidents reviewed by the undertaking; (b) remediation plans being implemented; (c) remediation plans that have been implemented, with results reviewed through routine internal management review processes; and (d) incidents no longer subject to action.
